llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Single AI treatment arm pooling all thirteen AI models of varying compute sizes; this is the only AI-assisted condition available and represents the overall effect of AI assistance versus no AI.
llm_extracted.notes: Sample sizes (n_human, n_ai) are not provided in the arms array. The primary comparison shows control mean of 600.7 seconds vs AI-assisted mean of 413.8 seconds, representing a 31.1% reduction in completion time (p=0.000). Additional statistics report scaling effects (12.3% reduction per 10x compute increase, 22.7% per GPT-jump) and skill-level heterogeneity, but these are secondary analyses. The reported_percent_change is negative (-0.311) because time decreased with AI assistance.
source_quotes: With access to any AI model this was reduced to 413.8 seconds (p=0.000), a statistically significant reduction in time of 31.1%.
Completed tasks without any AI assistance
Assigned one of thirteen AI models of differing compute sizes to aid them; all thirteen AI models are pooled into a single treatment group

Additional statistics report scaling effects (12.3% reduction per 10x compute increase, 22.7% per GPT-jump) and skill-level heterogeneity, but these are secondary analyses.
